user_content: I'm looking to get some advice on managing my time more efficiently. Lately, I've been working long hours on a high-priority project and my commute has increased, leaving me with little time for personal life. Do you have any tips on how to prioritize tasks and make the most out of my time?

I'll definitely try to implement these tips. One thing I'm struggling with is keeping track of my tasks and projects. I've been working professionally for 9 years and I'm currently using a physical notebook to jot down notes and reminders, but I'm not sure if that's the most efficient way. Do you have any recommendations for digital tools or apps that can help me stay organized?

i own a fractional leadership consulting company by the name of Sheba Consulting. I have a ton of experience as an executive but recently the economy has declined and with it my business. I did everything possible to reach new clients and it is too slow. what can you suggest i do to get some good new clients quickly?

That's really interesting! I've been thinking of sharing some of my farm products with friends and family, and the double-yolked eggs would make a great addition. Speaking of farm products, I've also been experimenting with making cheese from my goats' milk. Do you have any tips on how to age cheese properly?

I'm actually thinking of re-reading some old favorite books, including "Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone" and "The Perks of Being a Wallflower" by Stephen Chbosky. I've been re-reading them during my breaks at work, usually 10-15 minutes at a time.

I've been really enjoying the process of learning and improving my sculpting skills. Speaking of which, I was thinking of getting some new sculpting tools to help with the details on my current project. Do you have any recommendations for good online stores that sell sculpting tools and supplies?

I'm thinking of experimenting with different materials for my next project. I've been working with air-dry clay so far, but I'm curious about trying out polymer clay or maybe even wax. Do you have any tips or resources for working with these materials?

I'm a software engineer, specifically a backend developer, and I've been in this field since I graduated with a degree in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley. I've been working at NovaTech for about 4 years and 3 months now. Given my experience, I'm looking for a tool that's easy to use and can provide detailed insights into our API's performance. I've heard of New Relic and Datadog, but I'm not sure which one would be the best fit for our team. Can you give me a brief comparison of these two tools?

I think my team would greatly benefit from these tools, especially with the high-priority project we're working on at NovaTech. My top priority is to identify performance bottlenecks and optimize our API's response time, so I think I'll need a tool that provides detailed insights into transaction tracing and code-level profiling. I'll probably lean towards New Relic for this reason, but I'll definitely take a closer look at both tools and consider a trial or demo to make a final decision.

I'll start by saying that I'm a software engineer, specifically a backend developer, and I've been in this field since I graduated with a degree in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley. I've been working at NovaTech for about 4 years and 3 months now.

I'm considering using Google Drive since I already use Google Photos and it integrates well with it. But I'm not sure if I should upgrade to 100GB or 200GB. Do you think 100GB would be enough for my needs, or should I splurge on the 200GB plan?

I've been meaning to organize my music festival photos, but I haven't gotten around to it yet. I did use the auto mode on my phone's camera, but I didn't try any specific settings. I just pointed and shot! Maybe I'll try playing around with the camera settings next time.

And yeah, I've played around with Google Photos' editing features, but I haven't tried the automatic video creation yet. I'll have to check that out. It sounds like a cool way to create a quick recap of my festival experience.

I'm actually thinking of getting a new sculpting tool set specifically designed for detail work, something that can help me achieve finer textures and details on my life-sized cat sculpture. Do you have any recommendations for tool sets or brands that specialize in detail work? By the way, I've had my beginner's sculpting kit for about three weeks now, and I've been learning so much from it.
I'm excited to explore these new materials and techniques. Thanks for the tips and resources!
I'm struggling with profiling and benchmarking our API, can you recommend some tools for that?

Now that I've decided on the tool, I need to prepare a plan to implement it and get my team on board. Can you provide some guidance on how to roll out New Relic to my team and ensure a smooth integration with our existing workflows?
